insights: Category is the dominant driver of resolution duration variation, accounting for 44.2% of the median range, with Database tickets consistently 30.9% slower than the overall median—indicating a structural performance gap rather than a time-bound anomaly.
Resolution duration improved by 85.5% from Q1 to Q4 2023, with a linear trend of -408 hours per month, confirming strong departmental progress in operational efficiency over the year.

Database category underperformance persists across all quarters (18.0% average deviation above quarterly median), ranking as the slowest in Q2 and Q3 and showing increasing deviation from 16.4% in Q1–Q2 to 19.7% in Q3–Q4—highlighting an entrenched bottleneck requiring root-cause investigation.

Temporal seasonality reveals January and February as high-risk periods (59.6%–88.4% above annual median), while October–December are peak performance months (-70.7% to -73.5% below median), indicating recurring operational pressure at year-start that must be proactively managed.

Priority shows the weakest influence on resolution time (16.1% median range) and exhibits a counterintuitive pattern: Critical priority tickets have the longest median resolution time (3358.69 hours), suggesting misalignment between priority assignment and actual resource allocation or escalation handling.

Network tickets are the fastest-performing category (13.3% below median), and their performance peaks in Q3 (-51.4% below quarterly median) but deteriorates sharply in Q4 (+37.6%), revealing a category-specific temporal vulnerability that may reflect capacity saturation or process breakdown late in the year.
summary: The analysis confirms that goal completion *rates* cannot be assessed—since all 500 records are completed—but resolution *duration* serves as a robust proxy for departmental performance. Category is the strongest determinant of speed, with Database tickets exhibiting a consistent, structurally embedded slowdown that worsens toward year-end, signaling a critical operational bottleneck. Concurrently, the department achieved substantial year-over-year improvement (85.5% faster median resolution by Q4), though this progress is unevenly distributed: early-year months remain persistently weak, and priority-based triage appears ineffective—Critical tickets take longest to resolve. These findings point to two urgent focus areas: diagnosing and remediating Database-specific process constraints, and realigning priority protocols with actual resolution capacity—particularly during January–February and Q4.
